Riverside, CA  92507

                                    Re: Our Advice No. A-83-069

Dear Ms. Crouch:

     Thank you for your request for information on the filing of
Statements of Economic Interests (Form 730) by the consultants
who work for the Santa Ana Watershed Project Authority.
Specifically, the Authority contracted with Jones and Stokes
Associates, Inc., an environmental consulting firm, for the
preparation of an Environmental Impact Report. The company
indicated that it does not feel that the employees who work on
the project should be required to complete Statements. You
asked for guidance in determining when these, and other
consultants, must file Statements.

     The Political Reform Act requires a consultant to a local
government entity to be designated in the entity's code if the
consultant makes or participates in the making of decisions
which may foreseeably have a material effect on any financial
interests. (Government Code Section 87302l/.)

     A "consultant," within the meaning of the Act, includes "any
natural person who provides, under contract, information, advice,
recommendation or counsel to a local government agency..." (2 Cal.
Adm. Code Section 18700(a)(2), copy enclosed). This term does not
include, however, a person who:

     (A)  Conducts research and arrives at
conclusions with respect to his or her rendition
of information, advice, recommendation or counsel

_________________
1/  Hereinafter, all statutory references made are to
the Government Code unless otherwise specified.

Ms. Carolyn S. Crouch
March 22, 1983
Page Two


                 independent of the control and direction of the
                 agency or any agency official, other than normal
                 contract monitoring; and

                 (B)  Possesses no authority with respect to
                 any agency decision beyond the rendition of
                 any information, advice, recommendation or counsel.

                              2 Cal. Adm. Code Section
                              18700(a)(2)(A) and (B).

     When a person is hired under a contract to perform a
specific task, such as the preparation of an EIR, he or she
ordinarly comes within the exemption.  Under such circum-
stances, the individual uses his or her expertise to render
professional services according to the specifications of a
contract, and his or her judgment is not subject to review
or direction by the Authority.  Essentially, the individual
is being called upon to deliver a finished product, not
participate in the governmental decisions made by the
Authority.  Applying this analysis to what you have told us
of the contract with Jones and Stokes Associates, it does
not appear that the individuals who will be working on the
EIR must file Statements.2/

     On the other hand, if the Authority contracts with
consultants who do not satisfy all the criteria for the
exemption, they must be designated.  This determination
must be made on a contract-by-contract basis.  If you have
a question regarding a specific contract, please feel free
to contact our office for advice.

     I hope this information will be helpful to you.  Thank
you for your concern about this matter.

                              Very truly yours,

                              Janis Shank McLean
                              Janis Shank McLean
                              Counsel, Legal Division

February 6, 1980, the Commission for the Santa Ana Watershed Project
Authority has reviewed the list of consultants presently hired by
SAWPA and considered the range of their duties;

NOW, THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED that the Commissioners for the
Santa Ana Watershed Project Authority hereby declare the consultants
listed on Exhibit "A" attached hereto as "designated employees" who
are performing the duties described therein and find that they should
file disclosure statements in the appropriate categories as noted
on said Exhibit "A"; and

directed to transmit a copy of the Conflict of Interest Code to the
consultants, notifying them of their disclosure requirements, and
request that they file appropriate statements with the Santa Ana
Watershed Project Authority Filing Officer not later than April 25, 1980.

Dear Mr. Schlange:

     This letter is our proposal to prepare an Environmental Impact
Report for the new alignment of reaches IV-D and IV-E of the
Santa Ana Regional Interceptor. The proposed scope of work
is included as Attachment I.

     We propose to complete the Draft EIR and attend the Draft EIR
hearing for a cost not to exceed $12,400. We propose to pre-
pare the Final EIR for a cost not to exceed $700; this estimate
assumes the Final EIR will be a response-to-comments document.

     The above cost estimate does not include any on-site archeo-
logical surveys that could be required along the proposed
alignment. However, because construction would proceed within
existing rights-of-way where roads and canals have already
been constructed, it is unlikely such on-site surveys will
be necessary.

     We propose to complete the Preliminary Draft EIR within 45
days following authorization to proceed. The Draft EIR will
be completed within 7 days following receipt of SAWPA comments
on the Preliminary Draft EIR. The Final EIR will be completed
7 days following receipt of SAWPA comments on the Preliminary
Final EIR.

Page 2


Resumes of Jones & Stokes Associates staff members who would
be assigned to the project are included as Attachment II.
Mr. Albert Herson, the proposed project manager, has extensive
past experience in managing environmental impact assessments
of sewage projects. Mr. Thomas Wegge, a key staff environ-
mental planner proposed for the project, has gained considerable
familiarity with the SARI project through our 1980 review of
the environmental implications of changes in the SARI.

For your information, we have also included a statement of
Jones & Stokes Associates qualifications and experience as
Attachment III, and summaries of past related projects as
Attachment IV. We have had considerable experience working
with SAWPA and other local agencies in the SAWPA planning area,
and propose to use this experience to produce an EIR which
focuses on key project issues at the appropriate level of
detail.

We appreciate the opportunity of potentially working with SAWPA
again. If any questions arise regarding the proposal, feel
free to contact me or Al Herson.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Robert L. Jones

Attachments

Attachment I

SCOPE OF WORK

Task 1: Coordination

     Working contacts will be established with the client
and with other key agencies such as the Regional Water Quality
Control Board. Background documents and information will
be gathered to orient the study team; Jones & Stokes Associates
has available in its library most of the relevant project
planning and environmental assessment reports done for the
SARI. A coordination meeting will be held with SAWPA staff
and key consultants early in the EIR process.

Task 2: Refinement of Issues, Preparation of Initial Study
and Definition of Project and Alternatives

     The project-related environmental issues will be defined,
and an Initial Study checklist will be completed for inclusion
in the Notice of Preparation. Summary descriptions of the
proposed North Alignment of reaches IV-D and IV-E, and alter-
natives to this alignment, will be prepared, based on the
February 1982 alignment study.

Task 3: Institutional Constraints

     State and local government regulations and permit require-
ments that could affect the proposed project will be identified.

Task 4: Environmental Setting, Impacts, and Mitigation
Measures

     This task will focus on the construction and operational
impacts of the proposed alignment and alternatives, and ways
to mitigate these impacts. Since the proposed project is
a portion of the larger SARI project which has undergone
extensive previous environmental review, we propose wherever
possible to summarize or incorporate-by-reference the results
of previous environmental studies, noting any subsequent
changes that may have resulted from changes in the SARI project
design.

The construction and operational impacts that will be focussed upon include the following:

     • Construction Phase: traffic disruption, noise, archeological, biological, geology/soils, surface water and groundwater impacts. (The archeological assessment includes only an assessment of the general archeological sensitivity of the proposed alignment; if this assessment indicates onsite archeological surveys are needed in particular locations, the surveys could be added to the scope of work.)

     • Operational Phase: surface water, groundwater, energy and industrial growth-inducing impacts.

Task 5: Preliminary Draft EIR

     A preliminary Draft EIR will be prepared for SAWPA review.

Task 6: Draft EIR

     The Preliminary Draft EIR will be revised in response to SAWPA comments, and up to 50 copies of the Draft EIR will be printed. Additional copies can be printed at an estimated cost of $7/copy.

Task 7: Hearing

     The project manager and key technical staff will attend a hearing on the Draft EIR. The results of the Draft EIR will be summarized in a presentation and questions related to the Draft EIR will be responded to.

Task 8: Preliminary Final EIR

     A Preliminary Final EIR will be prepared for SAWPA review which responds to all comments received in the Draft EIR. No new major substantive research for the Preliminary Final EIR is included in our cost estimate.

Task 9: Prepare Final EIR

     The Preliminary Final EIR will be revised in response to SAWPA comments, and up to 50 copies of the Final EIR will be printed. Additional copies can be printed at an estimated cost of $3/copy.

Corporate Background 
Jones & Stokes Associates, Inc. was formed in 1970 to 
meet the growing need for responsible,scientific, environ-
mental expertise. 
The firm maintains a professional staff of 
approximately 25 persons offering diversified experience in 
such.disciplines as terrestrial and aquatic ecology, zoology, 
botany, wildlife management, fisheries biology, marine biology, 
envirolliuental engineering, water resources management, range 
management, forestry, geography, urban and regional planning, 
resource economics, recreational planning, air quality and 
noise analysis, and photo interpretation. 
Jones & Stokes Associates management and staff have 
broad experience working with legislative committees and many 
federal, state and local governmental agencies in natural 
resources management. 
Staff experience in the EIR/EIS process 
includes public agency and academic perspectives as well as 
the private consultant role. 
Involvement with citizen 
conservation groups over many years has provided Jones & Stokes 
Associates with insight into the goals, interests and concerns 
of conservation groups regarding the management of natural 
resources and maintenance of environmental quality. 
Members 
of the firm also have presented expert testimony in various 
legal and administrative proceedings, including those of the 
California Energy Commission, State Water Resources Control 
Board, and California Public utilities Commission. 
Members of the firm have extensive experience in 
organizing and managing interdisciplinary teams. 
The firm 
frequently establishes teams through subcontracts for special 
studies. 
Jones & Stokes also serves as a subcontractor to 
other firms. 
Jones & Stokes Associates has established and 
maintains an affirmative action/equal opportunity hiring program, 
and is a "mandatory employer" with special employment 
consideration given to Vietnam War veterans, and handicapped 
and disabled persons.

sections.question_synthetic: Must consultants working for a local government entity on specific projects like Environmental Impact Reports file Statements of Economic Interests under the Political Reform Act?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Consultants hired to perform specific technical tasks like preparing an EIR are generally exempt from filing Statements of Economic Interests if they conduct work independently and have no authority over agency decisions. However, this determination must be made on a contract-by-contract basis depending on whether the consultant satisfies all exemption criteria.

Crouch Santa Ana Watershed Project Authority 2060 Chicago Avenue, Suite C-3 Riverside, CA 92507 Re: Our Advice No. A-83-069 Dear Ms. Crouch: Thank you for your request for information on the filing of Statements of Economic Interests (Form 730) by the consultants who work for the Santa Ana Watershed Project Authority. Specifically, the Authority contracted with Jones and Stokes Associates, Inc., an environmental consulting firm, for the preparation of an Environmental Impact Report. The company indicated that it does not feel that the employees who work on the project should be required to complete Statements. You asked for guidance in determining when these, and other consultants, must file Statements. The Political Reform Act requires a consultant to a local government entity to be designated in the entity's code if the consultant makes or participates in the making of decisions which may foreseeably have a material effect on any financial interests. (Government Code Section 87302l/.) A "consultant," within the meaning of the Act, includes "any natural person who provides, under contract, information, advice, recommendation or counsel to a local government agency..." (2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18700(a)(2), copy enclosed). This term does not include, however, a person who: (A) Conducts research and arrives at conclusions with respect to his or her rendition of information, advice, recommendation or counsel _________________ 1/ Hereinafter, all statutory references made are to the Government Code unless otherwise specified. Ms. Carolyn S. Crouch March 22, 1983 Page Two independent of the control and direction of the agency or any agency official, other than normal contract monitoring; and (B) Possesses no authority with respect to any agency decision beyond the rendition of any information, advice, recommendation or counsel. 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18700(a)(2)(A) and (B). When a person is hired under a contract to perform a specific task, such as the preparation of an EIR, he or she ordinarly comes within the exemption. Under such circum- stances, the individual uses his or her expertise to render professional services according to the specifications of a contract, and his or her judgment is not subject to review or direction by the Authority. Essentially, the individual is being called upon to deliver a finished product, not participate in the governmental decisions made by the Authority. Applying this analysis to what you have told us of the contract with Jones and Stokes Associates, it does not appear that the individuals who will be working on the EIR must file Statements.2/ On the other hand, if the Authority contracts with consultants who do not satisfy all the criteria for the exemption, they must be designated. embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter responding to Santa Ana Watershed Project Authority's inquiry about whether consultants, specifically Jones and Stokes Associates employees working on an EIR, must file Statements of Economic Interests. The FPPC concluded that consultants performing specific contracted tasks are generally exempt from filing requirements if they work independently and lack decision-making authority.
